title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: Pi Agent: Potential XSS in HTML session exports via Markdown URL sanitization bypass
description:
# Potential XSS in HTML session exports via Markdown URL handling

Pi HTML exports render session Markdown into a static HTML file. Affected versions did not consistently reject unsafe Markdown link and image URL schemes. In versions with scheme filtering, C0 control characters in the URL scheme could bypass the check because browsers normalize those characters before navigation.

## Impact

The realistic attack path is indirect. An attacker would need to get suitable Markdown into a session, fo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for potential cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks through improperly sanitized Markdown URLs in HTML session exports. This could lead to the execution of malicious scripts in the context of the user's browser, potentially exposing sensitive session data or enabling phishing attacks.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of the software where the vulnerability is patched and URL scheme filtering is properly implemented.
Perform a thorough review of any exported HTML files for potentially malicious content before sharing or hosting them.
Educate users and developers about safe Markdown practices and the risks of XSS vulnerabilities in exported content.

title: Gitea: Token scope bypass on web archive download endpoint
description:
## Summary

PR #37698 added checkDownloadTokenScope to /raw/*, /media/*, and attachment download web endpoints. The /archive/* endpoint (repo.Download in routers/web/repo/repo.go:372) was not included in the fix. This endpoint accepts OAuth2 tokens via webAuth.AllowOAuth2 (registered at routers/web/web.go:1649-1652) but does not call checkDownloadTokenScope or CheckRepoScopedToken.

A personal access token with any non-repository scope (e.g., read:issue or read:misc) can download full repository

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-20706.

title: Gitea: Missing repository-unit authorization on issue-template API endpoints
description:
## Summary

Three Gitea API endpoints — `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issue_templates`,
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issue_config` and `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issue_config/validate`
— read files from the repository's **Code** default branch (`.gitea/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/*`
and `issue_config.yaml`) and return their contents, but are registered **without**
the `reqRepoReader(unit.TypeCode)` authorization middleware that every sibling
Code-tree endpoint in the same route group carries.

A user who has acce

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized users to access sensitive issue template files and configuration data from the repository's default branch. This could lead to information disclosure, enabling attackers to gain insights into internal processes or exploit the information for further attacks.
recommended_actions: Apply the patch or update to the latest version of Gitea that addresses CVE-2026-27783 to ensure proper authorization middleware is implemented.
Restrict access to repositories by enforcing strict access control policies and limiting exposure to trusted users only.
Monitor repository access logs for unusual or unauthorized access patterns to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: Gitea: Incomplete CVE-2025-68941 fix: /user/orgs missing checkTokenPublicOnly + switch-case logic flaw
description:
## Summary

Two related issues in the token public-only scope enforcement introduced by PR #32204 (CVE-2025-68941 fix). A public-only scoped API token can access private organization data.

## Issue 1: /user/orgs missing checkTokenPublicOnly()

`routers/api/v1/api.go` line 1599:
```go
m.Get("/user/orgs", reqToken(), tokenRequiresScopes(
    auth_model.AccessTokenScopeCategoryUser,
    auth_model.AccessTokenScopeCategoryOrganization,
), org.ListMyOrgs)
// Missing checkTokenPublicOnly()
```

Adjac

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-25714.

title: Gitea: Authorization Bypass via "Allow edits from maintainers" allows unauthorized commits to any readable repo
description:
## Summary

Any authenticated low-privilege user with read access to a repository can push arbitrary commits directly to that repository, bypassing all write-access checks.

## Vulnerability

Gitea's "Allow edits from maintainers" PR option can be abused via reverse-fork PRs:

1. The web UI PR-create endpoint binds `allow_maintainer_edit=true` **without** verifying that the submitter has write access to the HEAD repository.
2. Gitea allows creating a PR where **BASE = attacker's fork** and **HEA

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-26231.

title: Gitea: OAuth2 access token scope enforcement bypass via HTTP Basic authentication
description:
### Summary

Gitea fails to enforce OAuth2 access token scopes when the token is submitted via HTTP Basic authentication instead of a Bearer token. An OAuth2 application granted only `read:user` can use the same token as `Authorization: Basic base64(<token>:x-oauth-basic)` and perform write actions, including modifying profiles, adding email addresses, creating repositories, and deleting repositories as the authorizing user.

### Details

**Root cause:** `services/auth/basic.go` accepts OAuth2 a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to bypass OAuth2 access token scope restrictions when using HTTP Basic authentication. This can lead to unauthorized write actions, such as modifying user profiles, adding email addresses, creating repositories, and deleting repositories, potentially compromising sensitive data and operations.
recommended_actions: Apply the security patch or update to the latest version of Gitea that addresses CVE-2026-28699.
Restrict the use of OAuth2 tokens with HTTP Basic authentication by enforcing stricter access control policies.
Monitor and audit access logs for suspicious activity related to OAuth2 token usage.

title: Gogs: Overwriting critical files results in a denial of service
description:
**Vulnerability type:** Path Traversal
**Impact:** DoS
**Exploitation prerequisite:** authorized user
**Description:** As an authorized user, an intruder can dictate the value which is passed to the `git diff` command which, together with bypassing the filtering of the passed value, allows the user to bypass the target directory and write the result of the comparison to any arbitrary path.
**Researcher:** Artyom Kulakov (Positive Technologies)
**Mitigation:**
1. https://github.com/gogs/gogs/blob

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-52797.

title: Rclone: Unauthenticated command execution in `rclone rcd --rc-serve` via inline remote instantiation, bypassing CVE-2026-41179 fix
description:
## Summary

`rclone rcd --rc-serve` accepts unauthenticated `GET` and `HEAD` requests to paths of the form:

```text
/[remote:path]/object
```

The `remote` value is parsed from the URL and passed to normal backend initialization. Inline remote configuration can set backend options that execute local commands during initialization. As a result, a single unauthenticated `GET` or `HEAD` request can execute a command as the rclone process user.

Versions from 1.55.0 onwards are vulnerable to comman

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the host system running the vulnerable rclone process. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, privilege escalation, or complete system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately update rclone to a patched version (if available) or apply any mitigations provided by the vendor.
Disable the `rclone rcd --rc-serve` functionality if it is not strictly necessary for operations.
Implement strict network access controls to limit access to rclone's remote control interface, ensuring it is only accessible from trusted hosts.

title: @nuxt/webpack-builder and @nuxt/rspack-builder dev server same-origin check bypassed when Sec-Fetch-Site, Origin, and Referer are all absent (incomplete fix for GHSA-6m52-m754-pw2g)
description:
### Summary
This is an incomplete fix for [GHSA-6m52-m754-pw2g](https://github.com/nuxt/nuxt/security/advisories/GHSA-6m52-m754-pw2g). Source code may still be stolen during dev when using the webpack / rspack builder if the dev server is bound to a non-loopback address (e.g. `nuxt dev --host`) and the developer opens a malicious site on the same network.

### Details
The fix for [GHSA-6m52-m754-pw2g](https://github.com/nuxt/nuxt/security/advisories/GHSA-6m52-m754-pw2g) added an `Origin` / `Refe

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49993.

title: Cross-site scripting via <NoScript> slot content in Nuxt's head components
description:
### Impact

Nuxt's globally registered `<NoScript>` component (from `@unhead/vue` head components, re-exported by Nuxt) wrote its default-slot content to the `innerHTML` of the `<noscript>` head tag, bypassing the HTML escaping that `{{ }}` interpolation normally applies in Vue templates.

Applications that placed untrusted, attacker-controllable data inside a `<NoScript>` slot, for example:

```vue
<NoScript>{{ route.query.banner }}</NoScript>
```

would emit that value unescaped inside `<noscr

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: LiteLLM: Authentication Bypass via Host Header Injection
description:
### Impact

A Host-header parsing flaw in the LiteLLM proxy could, under specific conditions, allow unauthenticated access to protected management routes.

The auth layer derived the effective route from `request.url.path` in `litellm/proxy/auth/auth_utils.py::get_request_route()`, which Starlette reconstructs from the `Host` header. A crafted `Host` could therefore make the auth gate evaluate a different route from the one FastAPI dispatched.

**Most deployments are not affected.** The bypass i

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49468.

title: Gitea: Git Smart HTTP Skips Repository Token Scopes for Bearer Tokens
description:
### Summary
Gitea v1.26.1 enforces repository-scoped access-token permissions on repository operations. In the Git Smart HTTP path, however, this check runs only when the token is presented via HTTP Basic authentication — `CheckRepoScopedToken()` returns early unless `ctx.IsBasicAuth` is true — so the same token sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` bypasses the scope check entirely.

As a result, a PAT or OAuth2 token presented as a Bearer credential can clone or fetch private repositories wi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized access to private repositories when a Personal Access Token (PAT) or OAuth2 token is presented as a Bearer credential. This could lead to data leakage, intellectual property theft, or unauthorized code modifications, impacting the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive data.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Gitea that addresses CVE-2026-28744 to ensure proper enforcement of repository-scoped token permissions.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect unauthorized access attempts using Bearer tokens.
Review and rotate all existing PATs and OAuth2 tokens to mitigate potential misuse of compromised credentials.

title: n8n: SecurityScorecard Node Leaks API Token to User-Controlled Host
description:
## Impact
An authenticated user with permission to create or modify workflows and access to a SecurityScorecard credential with limited allowed domains could configure the SecurityScorecard node's report download operation to target an attacker-controlled URL. The node attached the SecurityScorecard API token to the outbound request, causing the credential to be sent to the attacker-controlled host bypassing credential configured limitations and exfiltrating.

## Patches
The issue has been fixed

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to exfiltrate API tokens from the SecurityScorecard node in n8n workflows, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data or systems. This could compromise the integrity and confidentiality of affected organizations' data.
recommended_actions: Immediately update n8n to the latest patched version to mitigate the vulnerability (check for the fix for CVE-2026-54304).
Audit existing workflows to identify and remove any potentially malicious configurations targeting attacker-controlled URLs.
Rotate all SecurityScorecard API tokens and any other credentials that may have been exposed.

title: n8n: MCP Browser HTTP Transport Exposes Unauthenticated Browser-Control Sessions
description:
## Impact
When `@n8n/mcp-browser` is run in HTTP transport mode, the MCP endpoint accepts session initialization and tool invocation requests without any authentication. Any network-reachable client, or any website visited by the user, can establish an MCP session and invoke browser-control tools.

Where the n8n AI Browser Bridge extension is installed and a browser connection is active, an unauthenticated caller can access browser-control capabilities including navigation, JavaScript evaluation

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54309.

title: n8n: Cross-Tenant Credential Takeover via Dynamic Credentials EE Endpoints
description:
## Impact
Three EE endpoints used by the Dynamic Credentials feature accepted any authenticated n8n session without performing per-resource ownership or scope checks on the target workflow or credential. An authenticated user with no project membership or credential sharing relationship could enumerate credential identifiers, names, and types referenced by any private workflow in the instance, initiate an OAuth authorization flow against another user's credential to overwrite its stored tokens w

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54305.

title: n8n: Credential Exfiltration via Permission Bypass
description:
## Impact
A member-level user with editor access to a shared workflow could reference credentials they do not own via specific public API endpoints. Credential ownership checks were only enforced partially leading to cross-user credential access.

This issue affects instances where workflow sharing is enabled and at least one workflow has been shared with a member-level user as an Editor.

## Patches
The issue has been fixed in n8n versions 1.123.55, 2.25.7, and 2.26.2. Users should upgrade to o

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54307.

title: n8n: Denial of Service via ZIP decompression in webhook workflow
description:
## Impact
The Compression node's Decompress operation expanded attacker-controlled archives into memory without enforcing limits on decompressed output size. An unauthenticated attacker could send a small compressed archive to a public webhook workflow using this node, causing the n8n process to terminate due to memory exhaustion and disrupting all workflows in the same instance. 

## Patches
The issue has been fixed in n8n version 2.24.0. Users should upgrade to this version or later to remedia

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to exploit the n8n Compression node's Decompress operation, leading to memory exhaustion and a denial of service (DoS) attack. This can disrupt all workflows running on the affected n8n instance, potentially impacting business operations reliant on automation workflows.
affected_industries: Any industry using n8n for workflow automation
recommended_actions: Upgrade to n8n version 2.24.0 or later to apply the patch that addresses this vulnerability.
Restrict access to public webhook endpoints to trusted sources using authentication or IP whitelisting.
Monitor memory usage of n8n instances and set up alerts for unusual spikes to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: n8n: Public API Execution Retry Authorization Bypass
description:
## Impact
The Public API endpoint for retrying executions authorized access using `workflow:read` rather than `workflow:execute`. An authenticated user with read-only access to a shared workflow could use the Public API to retry executions of that workflow, bypassing the intended permission boundary between read and execute access.

This issue affects instances where workflows are shared with other users or across projects.

## Patches
The issue has been fixed in n8n versions 2.25.7, and 2.26.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows authenticated users with read-only access to execute workflows without proper permissions, potentially leading to unauthorized actions, data manipulation, or unintended system behavior. The impact is limited to instances where workflows are shared with other users or across projects.
recommended_actions: Update n8n to the latest patched versions (2.25.7 or 2.26.2) immediately to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and audit user permissions for shared workflows to ensure that only trusted users have access.
Monitor API activity logs for any unauthorized execution retries and investigate suspicious actions.

title: n8n: Python Code Node AST Validator Bypass
description:
## Impact
An authenticated user with permission to create or modify workflows containing a Python Code node could bypass the AST security validator and access the task executor module namespace. On self-hosted instances where `N8N_BLOCK_RUNNER_ENV_ACCESS=false` is set, this extended to disclosure of environment variables accessible to the task runner process.

This issue only affects instances where the Python Task Runner is enabled and `N8N_BLOCK_RUNNER_ENV_ACCESS=true`.

## Patches
The issue h

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: n8n: Stored XSS in Chat Trigger Node
description:
## Impact
An authenticated user with workflow edit access could inject arbitrary JavaScript into the Chat Trigger's generated page by setting a malicious `webhookId`. When a logged-in user visited the chat URL, the injected code executed in the n8n origin with that user's session privileges.

## Patches
The issue has been fixed in n8n versions 1.123.55, 2.25.7, and 2.26.2. Users should upgrade to one of these versions or later to remediate the vulnerability.

## Workarounds
If upgrading is not i

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54302.

title: CVE-2026-41717 - Spring Data MongoDB contains a SpEL (Spring Expression Language) expression injection vulnerability.
description:
Spring Data MongoDB contains a SpEL (Spring Expression Language) expression injection vulnerability. The issue occurs during parameter binding when a user-defined repository query method is annotated with @Query and utilizes a capture-all placeholder.

Affected versions:
Spring Data MongoDB 5.0.0 through 5.0.5; 4.5.0 through 4.5.11; 4.4.0 through 4.4.14; 4.3.0 through 4.3.16; 4.2.0 through 4.2.15; 4.1.0 through 4.1.14; 4.0.0 through 4.0.15; 3.4.0 through 3.4.19.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit SpEL injection during parameter binding in user-defined repository query methods. This can lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or remote code execution in applications using vulnerable versions of Spring Data MongoDB.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest patched version of Spring Data MongoDB that addresses CVE-2026-41717.
Review and sanitize all @Query annotations in repository methods to ensure they do not expose sensitive data or allow injection.
Implement application-level monitoring and logging to detect and respond to suspicious query behavior.

title: CVE-2026-41716 - Spring Data's internal property-lookup cache accepts and permanently retains attacker-supplied strin
description:
Spring Data's internal property-lookup cache accepts and permanently retains attacker-supplied strings as cache keys, allowing heap exhaustion through repeated requests.

Affected versions:
Spring Data Commons 2.7.0 through 2.7.19; 3.3.0 through 3.3.16; 3.4.0 through 3.4.14; 3.5.0 through 3.5.11; 4.0.0 through 4.0.5.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41716.

title: CVE-2026-41714 - Applications that configure their broker connection via RabbitConnectionFactoryBean.setUri("amqps://
description:
Applications that configure their broker connection via RabbitConnectionFactoryBean.setUri("amqps://...") without also calling setUseSSL(true) get TLS encryption with no certificate validation and no hostname verification.

Affected versions:
Spring AMQP 4.0.0 through 4.0.3; 3.2.0 through 3.2.10; 3.1.0 through 3.1.15; 2.4.0 through 2.4.17.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for insecure TLS connections due to the lack of certificate validation and hostname verification. This could enable man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks, leading to potential data interception, unauthorized access, or data tampering.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched version of Spring AMQP that addresses CVE-2026-41714.
Ensure that applications explicitly call setUseSSL(true) when configuring broker connections using RabbitConnectionFactoryBean.setUri.
Conduct a review of all RabbitMQ configurations to verify proper certificate validation and hostname verification settings.

title: CVE-2026-41711 - Applications using Spring Data Commons may be vulnerable to a Denial of Service (DoS) attack leading
description:
Applications using Spring Data Commons may be vulnerable to a Denial of Service (DoS) attack leading to a StackOverflowException when parsing Sort parameters.

Affected versions:
Spring Data Commons 4.0.0 through 4.0.5; 3.5.0 through 3.5.11; 3.4.0 through 3.4.14; 3.3.0 through 3.3.16; 3.2.0 through 3.2.15; 3.1.0 through 3.1.14; 3.0.0 through 3.0.15; 2.7.0 through 2.7.19.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41711.

title: CVE-2026-41706 - Spring Security's CookieRequestCache and CookieServerRequestCache store the pre-authentication reque
description:
Spring Security's CookieRequestCache and CookieServerRequestCache store the pre-authentication request URL in a browser cookie so that users can be redirected back to their intended destination after a successful login. In affected versions, the full absolute URL is stored in the cookie and is used without validation as the post-login redirect target.

Affected versions:
Spring Security 5.7.0 through 5.7.23; 5.8.0 through 5.8.25; 6.3.0 through 6.3.16; 6.4.0 through 6.4.16; 6.5.0 through 6.5.10; 7.0.0 through 7.0.5.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41706.

title: CVE-2026-41701 - Correlation IDs for replies in the RabbitTemplate.sendAndReceive() with the fixed reply queue are pr
description:
Correlation IDs for replies in the RabbitTemplate.sendAndReceive() with the fixed reply queue are predictable due to internal simple counter.

Affected versions:
Spring AMQP 4.0.0 through 4.0.3; 3.2.0 through 3.2.10; 3.1.0 through 3.1.15; 2.4.0 through 2.4.17.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41701.

title: CVE-2026-41697 - Spring Data Relational does not properly escape binding values of externally-controlled input when u
description:
Spring Data Relational does not properly escape binding values of externally-controlled input when using StringMatcher (STARTING, ENDING, or CONTAINING) in Query By Example (QBE). An attacker can supply wildcard characters to perform boolean-based blind data inference.

Affected versions:
Spring Data Relational/JDBC/R2DBC 4.0.0 through 4.0.5; 3.5.0 through 3.5.11; 3.4.0 through 3.4.14; 3.3.0 through 3.3.16; 3.2.0 through 3.2.15; 3.1.0 through 3.1.14; 3.0.0 through 3.0.15; 2.4.0 through 2.4.19.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41697.

title: CVE-2026-41696 - Spring Data MongoDB repository query methods annotated with @Query that use regex parameter binding
description:
Spring Data MongoDB repository query methods annotated with @Query that use regex parameter binding perform insufficient validation of the bound parameter. An attacker can supply a crafted string to break out of the intended regular expression quoting.

Affected versions:
Spring Data MongoDB 5.0.0 through 5.0.5; 4.5.0 through 4.5.11; 4.4.0 through 4.4.14; 4.3.0 through 4.3.16; 4.2.0 through 4.2.15; 4.1.0 through 4.1.14; 4.0.0 through 4.0.15; 3.4.0 through 3.4.19.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41696.

title: CVE-2026-41695 - Spring Data Commons applications may be vulnerable to denial of service through resource exhaustion
description:
Spring Data Commons applications may be vulnerable to denial of service through resource exhaustion when attacker-controlled property path strings are passed to MappingContext property path resolution.

Affected versions:
Spring Data Commons 4.0.0 through 4.0.5; 3.5.0 through 3.5.11; 3.4.0 through 3.4.14.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41695.

title: CVE-2026-41694 - Since Spring Security SAML decrypts SAML Responses as well as elements of SAML LogoutRequests and Lo
description:
Since Spring Security SAML decrypts SAML Responses as well as elements of SAML LogoutRequests and LogoutResponses without requiring a valid signature, attackers may be able to craft these SAML payloads and use the Service Provider as a decryption oracle.

Affected versions:
Spring Security 5.7.0 through 5.7.23; 5.8.0 through 5.8.25; 6.3.0 through 6.3.16; 6.4.0 through 6.4.16; 6.5.0 through 6.5.10; 7.0.0 through 7.0.5.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41694.

title: CVE-2026-41008 - Spring Security Authorization Server's authorization endpoint performs insufficient validation of th
description:
Spring Security Authorization Server's authorization endpoint performs insufficient validation of the request_uri parameter. An attacker can craft a malicious authorization request containing an invalid request_uri and an arbitrary, unvalidated redirect_uri, which can lead to an Open Redirect vulnerability.

Affected versions:
Spring Security 7.0.0 through 7.0.5.
Spring Authorization Server 1.5.0 through 1.5.7.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41008.

title: CVE-2026-41003 - An attacker able to influence values in RelyingPartyRegistration may be able to run arbitrary code o
description:
An attacker able to influence values in RelyingPartyRegistration may be able to run arbitrary code on HTML forms generated by Spring Security filters.

Affected versions:
Spring Security 5.7.0 through 5.7.23; 5.8.0 through 5.8.25; 6.3.0 through 6.3.16; 6.4.0 through 6.4.16; 6.5.0 through 6.5.10; 7.0.0 through 7.0.5.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41003.

title: CVE-2026-40993 - An attacker with write permissions to the database table managed by JdbcAssertingPartyMetadataReposi
description:
An attacker with write permissions to the database table managed by JdbcAssertingPartyMetadataRepository (saml2_asserting_party_metadata) may be able to store malicious serialized payloads in the columns containing the collection of verification or encryption credentials (verification_credentials and encryption_credentials, respectively).

Affected versions:
Spring Security 7.0.0 through 7.0.5.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40993.

title: CVE-2026-40991 - When using spring-restdocs-webtestclient or spring-restdocs-restassured to document a remote API acc
description:
When using spring-restdocs-webtestclient or spring-restdocs-restassured to document a remote API accessed over HTTP, an attacker who compromises the API or tricks the user into documenting a malicious API can perform an XXE injection attack when the documentation-generating tests are next executed.

Affected versions:
Spring REST Docs 4.0.0; 3.0.0 through 3.0.5; 2.0.0.RELEASE through 2.0.8.RELEASE.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40991.

title: CVE-2026-40988 - An application using spring-security-saml2-service-provider and the REDIRECT binding for SAML 2.0 Lo
description:
An application using spring-security-saml2-service-provider and the REDIRECT binding for SAML 2.0 Login or Logout may be vulnerable to a denial of service by way of an unbounded writer that inflates the compressed SAML payload into memory.

Affected versions:
Spring Security 5.7.0 through 5.7.23; 5.8.0 through 5.8.25; 6.3.0 through 6.3.16; 6.4.0 through 6.4.16; 6.5.0 through 6.5.10; 7.0.0 through 7.0.5.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40988.
